member.office_address: 2108 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0548

form_analysis.form_header_text: Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations Request Form- Programmatic and Language Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Please provide detailed information regarding the location of your request in the subcommittee bill or report, down to the activity and account. After submission, if there are changes or updates to the information, please advise the staff responsible for the changes. Please be advised that staff may reach out with additional questions should the Appropriations Committee require additional information on a submission, both before and after the submission deadline. Types of Requests: There are two types of requests that Congressman Issa will be submitting to the House Committee on Appropriations: Programmatic - general support or funding for a program. Language - a request to add language to the bill or report to accompany the bill which generally does not include dollar amounts. If you have any questions about your request, please email Giulia.DiGuglielmo@Mail.House.Gov. If you have any additional questions or need assistance with another matter, please call 202-225-5672.

sections.description: Details regarding the requesting organization and points of contact.

fields.label: Requesting Organization:
Local Point of Contact / Email / Phone Number:
fields.help_text: Please provide name, email address, and phone number for the local contact.

fields.help_text: If you have a Washington, DC representative, provide their name and contact details.

fields.label: DC POC's Organization/Firm (if applicable)

sections.description: General information about the proposal and its classification.

fields.label: Proposal Title:
fields.rationale: Standardized title for programmatic funding requests targeting the Science account.

fields.label: Type of Request:
fields.help_text: Programmatic requests involve funding for a program; Language requests involve adding text to the bill or report.
fields.rationale: The reference values explicitly state that this is a programmatic funding request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Subcommittee
fields.help_text: Select the relevant House Appropriations Subcommittee.
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee, as confirmed in the reference values.

fields.label: If you are submitting more than one request, please list priority of this request (1 of 3 total, 2 of 4 total, etc.)
fields.help_text: Indicate the rank and total number of requests submitted by your organization.
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific prioritization question that depends on the submitter's full slate of requests.

fields.label: Which Member of Congress is leading this request?
fields.rationale: The member for the specified district (CA-48) is the lead for this constituent submission.

sections.description: Detailed justification for the request including community impact and history.

fields.label: Please clearly state what this request would do in 1-2 sentences.
fields.help_text: Provide a brief summary of the request purpose.
fields.draft_value:
* This request provides $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to advance U.S. leadership in planetary science, astrophysics, heliophysics, and Earth science.
* Funding supports the execution of peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities and ensures the stability of the highly specialized aerospace workforce.
fields.rationale: Directly reflects the $9 billion programmatic ask for the SMD and incorporates advocacy framing regarding leadership and workforce stability.

fields.label: What is the underlying problem/issue, and how will this request solve or address it?
fields.draft_value:
* Recent budget volatility and proposed cuts in FY 2026 threatened critical mission timelines and led to thousands of contractor layoffs and civil servant departures.
* China is rapidly expanding its space science ambitions to set exploration norms; this $9 billion request restores the investment level required to maintain American preeminence.
* Sustained funding provides the certainty needed for long-term mission planning and prevents the loss of hard-won technical capabilities across the industrial base.
fields.rationale: Utilizes advocacy talking points regarding the space race with China and workforce disruption while providing the $9 billion solution.

fields.label: Describe how this proposal will benefit the 48th Congressional District and/or State of California:
fields.draft_value:
* Supports local high-tech manufacturing at Data Device Corp in CA-48, which has received approximately $16,978 in NASA Science contract obligations since FY2023.
* Sustains California's position as a global leader in aerospace innovation, supporting thousands of jobs at NASA centers (JPL and Ames) and throughout the statewide supply chain.
* Catalyzes technology transfers and commercial spinoffs within California's innovation economy, delivering a significant return on federal investment.
fields.rationale: Cites specific spending data for Data Device Corp in CA-48 and connects it to the broader California aerospace ecosystem.

fields.label: Has this topic/item been discussed in a public setting, including a city council meeting or public forum?
fields.draft_value: Yes, NASA Science funding is a matter of significant public interest and is frequently discussed in congressional hearings and scientific community forums.
fields.rationale: NASA funding is a standard topic of public discourse in both legislative and scientific communities.

fields.label: Did you make this same request previously? Was your request included in the funding legislation?
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding prior submission history which is not available in the provided context.

fields.label: Describe how this proposal is a good use of taxpayer dollars:
fields.draft_value:
* Every dollar invested in NASA returns roughly three dollars to the economy through technological innovation and industrial growth.
* NASA Science represents less than one-third of one percent of federal spending while delivering vital services like weather forecasting and planetary defense.
* These missions inspire the next generation of STEM professionals, strengthening the talent pipeline for the broader aerospace and technology sectors.
fields.rationale: Incorporates pre-computed advocacy talking points regarding economic return and societal value.

fields.label: Please list other Members of Congress and/or Senator to whom you are submitting this request.
fields.draft_value:
* Reps.
* Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

sections.description: Specific account information and financial data for the request.

fields.label: What is the name of the program and which executive agency is responsible for it?
fields.rationale: As specified in the reference values for Agency and Account Name.

fields.label: Please provide the department, agency, and specific account that funds this program:
fields.rationale: Identifies the agency and account according to the reference data.

fields.label: If this is a defense-focused request, please provide the specific account and PE/Line Number for the program here:
fields.rationale: This is a civil space request funded under the CJS bill, not a defense request.

fields.label: What is the amount you are requesting for FY27?
fields.help_text: Enter the specific dollar amount requested for the 2027 fiscal year.
fields.rationale: Matches the exact Funding Ask provided in the reference values.

fields.label: What is the President's FY27 Budget Request for this item?
fields.help_text: Enter the amount requested by the President in the FY27 budget proposal.
fields.rationale: The reference values state that the FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released.

fields.label: What was the FY26 enacted amount for this request?
fields.help_text: Enter the numeric dollar amount enacted in the previous fiscal year.
fields.rationale: Matches the Enacted Total provided in the reference values for FY2026.

fields.label: Please share recent funding history for your program. List funding levels for the past 3-5 years.
fields.help_text: Provide historical funding data in narrative or list format.
fields.draft_value:
* FY2025 Enacted: $7.334 billion
* FY2026 Enacted: $7.250 billion
* FY2027 Programmatic Request: $9.000 billion
fields.rationale: Uses authoritative topline funding history from the reference values.
